title: centratherum camporum
intro: Centratherum camporum, commonly known as the Wild Celery or Kandulu, is a tropical plant species native to the tropical regions of Africa and Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties and has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Centratherum camporum include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its therapeutic actions, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects.
parts: Centratherum camporum has many medicinal parts, such as leaves, roots, and flowers, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The leaves of Centratherum camporum contain flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The roots of this plant contain sesquiterpenes, including centratherol and camporol, which have been shown to exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities. The flowers of Centratherum camporum contain essential oils, including geraniol and linalool, which have been used to treat respiratory issues and promote relaxation.
side_effects: Centratherum camporum can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory problems in some individuals. Prolonged exposure to the plant's oils may lead to inflammation, redness, and blisters on the skin. Ingesting the plant's leaves or flowers can cause nausea, vomiting, and stomach cramps, and may interact with certain medications. In rare cases, improper use of Centratherum camporum may also trigger anaphylaxis, a severe and life-threatening allergic reaction.
